mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate tightens labor markets, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in declining UNRATE periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rate crossing above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising industrial production and falling housing starts signals a rotation from cyclical construction to manufacturing output, which directly precedes positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as corporate capex accelerates.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the joint Rank IC drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase, a rapid increase in non-farm payrolls combined with unemployment falling below 4.0% signals strong labor demand that lifts consumer spending expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward IC remains negative over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns in high-payroll months are not statistically distinct from baseline (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth indicates expanding aggregate demand that directly supports corporate revenue projections, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in rising GDP quarters are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 300.0, inflation expectations become unanchored, prompting equity risk premium expansion and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite CPI crossing the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in consumer credit outstanding and falling unemployment reflects a credit-fueled consumption boom that historically peaks before equity corrections, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the joint Rank IC drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle where the federal funds rate is rising, a contraction in industrial production signals supply-side bottlenecks turning into demand destruction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward IC remains positive over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns in tightening periods with falling INDPRO are not significantly lower than baseline.
